title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Lord Runciman informs the Marquess of Carrington that a memoir was secretly moved weeks before a man's death. The manuscripts were sent to James McGregor, a Canadian currently living in Africa, who is scheduled to arrive on the *Grangewood* this Thursday. Runciman intends to meet McGregor to request a one-month delay in the memoir's publication and to carefully edit the text. The Earl of Carringham opposes inviting McGregor to the Chimney House due to a personal dislike of Canadians living in Africa. 

Roxane suggests employing a clever, attractive woman to handle McGregor to avoid angering him. After dismissing several candidates, Runciman proposes his cousin, Virginia Rue, noting her personal charm and her familiarity with Hesse-Slav due to her husband's embassy work. The Earl of Carringham agrees, finding the prospect of seeing Virginia Ruef appealing.

Later, at Whitehall, George Roxton (referred to as George Romax) asks his assistant, Bill Everleigh, to check Mrs. Rue's telephone number at 487 Pound Street. Upon learning her phone line is out, Roxton instructs Bill to take a taxi to her home to ask if she will be available at four o'clock that afternoon for an important discussion. Bill arrives at the residence and meets Virginia Ruef, a charming twenty-seven-year-old woman. Virginia, who had lied to Bill about her phone being broken to facilitate a visit, mentions she is preparing to go to Sloane Street to purchase a new red rubber garter belt.
